properties.remark: REPORT OF HOME DESTROYED, FAMILY WAS UNINJURED BECAUSE THEY WERE IN STORM SHELTER.

THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE OF 75-85 MPH WAS FOUND ACROSS FAR NORTHWESTERN VAN BUREN COUNTY NEAR AND SOUTH OF THE TOWN OF LESLIE. NUMEROUS TREES WERE DOWNED, AND THERE WAS DAMAGE TO THE ROOFS OF A COUPLE OF HOMES AND A SMALL COMMERCIAL BUILDING.

ARDOT REPORTS HWY 13 NORTH OF MCRAE CLOSED DUE TO HIGH WATER. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

A LARGE SWATH OF THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE WAS FOUND FROM 7.5 MILES SOUTHWEST OF MONTICELLO MOVING SOUTHEAST, ENDING NEAR THE TOWN OF JEROME. DAMAGE TO TREES AND HOMES IN THIS PATH WERE FOUND TO BE CONSISTENT WITH 80 TO 95 MPH WINDS.
THE RAWS SITE AT THE UNIVERSITY OF ARKANSAS AT MONTICELLO MEASURED A 71 MPH GUST.
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWNED IN ST. CHARLES.
POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 1 AT WATSON.

A BRIEF EF-1 TORNADO WAS FOUND JUST SOUTH OF THE TOWN OF JEROME. THE TORNADO STRUCK A SERIES OF LARGE INDUSTRIAL GRAIN AND FEED SILOS AND CAUSED SUBSTANTIAL DAMAGE. ONE OF THE SILOS HAD ITS FOUNDATION RIPPED OUT OF THE GROUND AND IT WAS BOLTED INTO CONCRETE IN SEVERAL PLACES. THE ROOF OF ANOTHER LARGE SILO WAS BLOWN DOWNWIND AT LEAST A HALF MILE AND FELL APART INTO PIECES. ELECTRICAL POLES WERE SNAPPED AND TREES WERE SNAPPED AT THE TRUNK OR UPROOTED ALONG AN APPROXIMATELY 0.8 MILE LONG PATH.
ARDOT REPORTS HWY 62 AND HWY 175 NEAR CHEROKEE VILLAGE FLOODED. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 1 AT WATSON.

ARDOT REPORTS HWY 115 NEAR JESUP CLOSED DUE TO HIGH WATER. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
ARDOT REPORTS ALL DIRECTIONS OF HWY 166 AT MILE MARKER 3 CLOSED DUE TO HIGH WATER. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
ARDOT REPORTS HWY 83 AND HWY 54 SOUTH OF GOULD ARE CLOSED DUE TO HIGH WATER. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
DAMAGE TO CARPORTS AND OUTBUILDINGS WERE OBSERVED. WINDS ESTIMATED AT 50-55 MPH.
NUMEROUS LOW WATER CROSSINGS AND BRIDGES THROUGHOUT NORTHWESTERN YELL COUNTY HAVE BEEN INUNDATED WITH WATER AND ARE IMPASSABLE.
SEVERAL LOW WATER CROSSINGS ARE FLOODED IN SOUTHEASTERN SCOTT COUNTY.
DUE TO EXTENSIVE FLOODING, SEVERAL ROADS IN SALINE COUNTY WERE CLOSED, ESPECIALLY NEAR THE TRASKWOOD AND TULL AREAS AND UP TO THE GRANT COUNTY LINE. WATER WAS MOVING SWIFTLY OVER SOME ROADWAYS.
FLOODED ROADS PREVENTED DELIVERY OF NEWSPAPERS.

ONE INCH DIAMETER HAIL REPORTED BETWEEN BIGELOW AND WYE MOUNTAIN. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR AND REPORT RELAYED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
DAMAGE WAS REPORTED TO A HOUSE AND DOWNED POWER LINES EAST OF VALLEY SPRINGS.

AN EF-2 TORNADO TRACKED FROM 2 MILES SOUTHWEST OF EVERTON TO 3 MILES WSW OF YELLVILLE. THE PATH WAS 500 YARDS WIDE AND 12.5 MILES LONG. TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED, POWER POLES BROKEN, OUTBUILDINGS SEVERELY DAMAGED OR DESTROYED, AND SEVERAL RESIDENCES SUSTAINED SERIOUS DAMAGE. THE BRUNO-PYATT SCHOOL SUSTAINED DAMAGE. ONE PERSON HAD A MINOR INJURY IN MARION COUNTY.
THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE IN LITTLE ROCK HAS CONFIRMED AN EF2 TORNADO IN PORTIONS OF BOONE AND MARION COUNTIES IN NORTHWEST ARKANSAS.
THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE IN LITTLE ROCK HAS CONFIRMED A TORNADO IN PORTIONS OF BOONE AND NEWTON COUNTIES IN NORTHWEST ARKANSAS.
DAMAGE WAS REPORTED TO A HOME IN EVERTON.
DAMAGE WAS REPORTED AT BRUNO-PYATT SCHOOL.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF-1 TORNADO THAT BEGAN 3 MILES NNE OF GASSVILLE AND ENDED 3 MILES W OF MOUNTAIN HOME. THE DAMAGE PATH WAS 600 YARDS WIDE AND 2.16 MILES LONG. SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED, WITH OUTBUILDINGS DAMAGED. A LARGE TREE WAS UPROOTED AND FELL THROUGH THE BEDROOM OF A RESIDENCE. A LARGE PORTION OF THE ROOF OF A HOUSE WAS TORN OFF AND BLOWN INTO NEARBY TREES.

AN EF-1 TORNADO TRACKED FROM 4 MILES NORTH-NORTHEAST OF HECTOR TO 5 MILES NORTH-NORTHEAST OF HECTOR. THE PATH WAS 300 YARDS WIDE AND .81 MILES LONG. THIS TORNADO BEGAN NEAR THE HIGHWAY 27 BRIDGE OVER THE MIDDLE FORK OF ILLINOIS BAYOU. IT UPROOTED AND SNAPPED SEVERAL TREES.

AN EF-1 TORNADO TRACKED FROM 11 MILES NORTHEAST OF HECTOR TO 14 MILES NORTHEAST OF HECTOR. THE PATH WAS 200 YARDS WIDE AND 2.8 MILES LONG. TREES WERE UPROOTED OR DOWNED AND A TREE WAS BLOWN ONTO A BARN. SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED.
ONE INCH DIAMETER HAIL FELL NEAR THE CLINTON AIRPORT.
NUMEROUS DOWNED TREES CAUSED WIDESPREAD POWER OUTAGES IN SHARP COUNTY.

AN EF-1 TORNADO TRACKED FROM 4 MILES NORTHEAST OF EVENING SHADE TO 9 MILES EAST-NORTHEAST OF EVENING SHADE. THE PATH WAS 400 YARDS WIDE AND 5.67 MILES LONG. TREES AND POWERLINES WERE BLOWN OVER AND SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED.

FLASH FLOODING WAS STARTING TO IMPACT SEVERAL COUNTY ROADS NEAR GREENBRIER, REQUIRING THE LOCAL FIRE DEPARTMENT TO DEPLOY BARRICADES. THE MOST HIGHLY TRAVELED ROAD IMPACTED WAS LINDOR RD. BEFORE HIGHWAY 65.

ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF SHIRLEY, SILVER ROCK ROAD WAS FLOODED. WEAVER CREEK WAS ALSO OUT OF ITS BANKS IN EASTERN VAN BUREN COUNTY. OTHER COMMONLY FLOODED LOW-LYING AREAS WERE FLOODED IN EASTERN VAN BUREN COUNTY.
EXTENSIVE STREET FLOODING ON GRAND AVE IN HOT SPRINGS.
OWLY ROAD WAS FLOODED, AND WILLIAMS CREEK HAD COME OUT OF ITS BANK AND WAS RUNNING OVER THE BRIDGE NEARBY.
HIGHWAYS 8 AND 26 WERE IMPASSABLE DUE TO FLOODING 5 MILES WEST OF ARKADELPHIA AND 5 MILES SOUTHWEST OF ARKADELPHIA, RESPECTIVELY.

THIS REPORT IS AN UPDATE OF A STORM REPORT FROM SEVERAL DAYS AGO. AT THE CRYSTAL SPRINGS RESORT, STRONG THUNDERSTORM WINDS BLEW MOST OF THE TIN ROOFING OFF OF ONE OF THE PRIMARY DOCKS, SET A FEW BOATS LOOSE IN THE LAKE AND UPROOTED OR SNAPPED SEVERAL TREES IN THE AREA. WHILE THE MOST EXTENSIVE DAMAGE OCCURRED AT ONE DOCK, A FEW OTHER DOCKS WERE PUSHED TOGETHER RESULTING IN SOME MINOR DAMAGE TO THE DOCKS AND BOATS IN BETWEEN.
TREES WERE REPORTED DOWN ON HIGHWAY 27, SOUTH OF MOUNT IDA.
HIGHWAY 71 AT Y CITY IS CLOSED DUE TO HIGH WATER.
CULVERTS ALONG OLD DALLAS RD WERE WASHED OUT DUE TO HIGH WATER.
SEVERAL COUNTY ROADS AND HIGHWAYS WERE IMPASSABLE DUE TO HIGH WATER DURING THE OVERNIGHT HOURS.
A TREE WAS BLOWN DOWN.
A FEW TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN IN THE AREA WITH POWER OUTAGES IN THIS LOCATION AS WELL.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL WAS NOTED ABOUT FOUR MILES NORTHEAST OF DES ARC.

THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED ACROSS PORTIONS OF EASTERN WOODRUFF COUNTY AS NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG WITH SEVERAL POWERPOLES. A FEW HOMES RECEIVED MINOR DAMAGE DUE TO TREES FALLING ON OR NEXT TO THEM. ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEEDS WERE IN THE 70-80 MPH RANGE.

PER THE MONTGOMERY COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE, MULTIPLE TENTS AND ONE VEHICLE WERE WASHED INTO THE CADDO RIVER NEAR THE TOWN OF NORMAN. ALL CAMPERS EVACUATED BEFOREHAND AND THERE ARE NO REPORTS OF INJURIES AT THIS TIME.
PER THE PIKE COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE, MULTIPLE VEHICLES NEAR THE CADDO RIVER WERE WASHED AWAY DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. THERE WERE ALSO FLOODED ROADS IN AND NEAR THE TOWN OF GLENWOOD.
A LARGE TREE WAS BLOWN DOWN ON A HOME. WINDS FROM AN OBSERVATION SITE NEAR TOWN MEASURED BETWEEN 45 AND 50 MPH AROUND THAT TIME.

SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AT SHERIDAN.

STORM SURVEY REVEALED A NARROW SWATH OF STRAIGHT-LINE WIND DAMAGE IN AN AREA ABOUT 5 MILES NORTH OF CLARKSVILLE...MAINLY JUST NORTH OF LUDWIG LAKE. SOME SMALL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED...WITH SEVERAL TREES EITHER SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. THIS DAMAGE WAS CONSISTENT WITH WINDS AROUND 70 TO 75 MPH.
LAW ENFORCEMENT CONFIRMED FLOODING ON PORTIONS OF 226 AND HIGH WATER BEGINNING TO IMPACT HIGHWAY 157 IN DIAZ.
COOPERATIVE OBSERVER REPORTED HIGH WATER IN AND NEAR THE WASTE WATER FACILITY IMPACTING SEVERAL ROADS.
**THIS IS A DELAYED REPORT FROM THIS MORNING.** A RESIDENT OF JACKSONPORT MEASURED 3.60 OF RAIN WITHIN 2 HOURS THIS MORNING. SHE ALSO REPORTED FLASH FLOODING WITH WATER FLOODING HER GARAGE AND SHED.
ARDOT REPORTED SECTIONS OF HIGHWAYS 37 AND 226 ARE CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING.

A SURVEY CREW FROM THE NWS OFFICE IN LITTLE ROCK FOUND TORNADO DAMAGE CONSISTANT WITH AN EF0 RATING. THE TORNADO FORMED JUST NORTHEAST OF THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAYS 37 AND 269 NORTH OF MCCRORY AND TRAVELED ABOUT A MILE AND A QUARTER TO THE NORTHWEST BEFORE LIFTING. A SURVEY CREW FROM THE NWS OFFICE IN LITTLE ROCK FOUND TORNADO DAMAGE CONSISTANT WITH AN EF2 RATING. A HOUSE SUSTAINED MAJOR ROOF DAMAGE, WITH MOST OF THE WALLS STILL STANDING. ADDITIONAL INFORMATION IS STILL BEING GATHERED AND A DETAILED SUMMARY RAGARDING THE TRACK OF THE STORM WILL BE ISSUED BY THIS EVENING.

SURVEY RESULTS REVEALED AN EF2 TORNADO SOUTH OF HEBER SPRINGS. THIS TORNADO FORMED NEAR CANDLESTICK ROAD EAST OF HWY 5, DAMAGING SEVERAL TREES AND AN OUTBUILDING. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST DAMAGING MORE TREES AND OUTBUILDINGS ALONG BLAKEY CIRCLE AND BLAKEY ROAD. THE TORNADO THEN CONTINUED NORTHEAST CROSSING THE LITTLE RED RIVER ALONG WILDFLOWER ROAD, SNAPPING MANY TREES HEALTHY TREES. THIS IS WHERE THE STRONGEST WINDS WERE NOTED. THE TORNADO LIFTED SHORTLY AFTER THIS LOCATION.
